_warning: HAND-AUTHORED. A sync script that overwrites this file is a bug. verified:false means Shawn has not confirmed the chapter stamps against the manga yet.
_why_this_file_exists: No upstream source records WHERE a crew or character is AS OF a chapter. The API gives rosters and bounties as single current-day values; it has no location and no time axis. Presence is therefore authored: each entity carries an ordered list of windows, and the active window at a chapter is the LAST one whose from_chapter <= the reader's chapter — unless that window has a non-null to_chapter the reader has passed, in which case the entity is off the map (a death, an arrest, a disbandment, a departure). Same shape as vessels.json's last-before-chapter gate, plus an ending.
_coord_resolution: Each window resolves its position from canon/islands.coords.json by island_slug, OR carries an explicit lng/lat (used for open-sea anchorages, Thriller Bark — a ship, not an island — and Mary Geoise). scripts/normalize.py FAILS LOUD on a slug that resolves to no coordinate.
_confidence_semantics: canon_confidence on a window rates the POSITION claim, exactly like an island pin: canon = the story puts them there in those chapters; derived = a home-base/affiliation inference; guess = a representative open-sea anchorage, honest set dressing.
_excluded_on_purpose: Roger Pirates — disbanded before chapter 1. A defunct crew has no presence. check_canon.py asserts they never appear here.
_before_any_content_ships: Shawn must confirm every window and member chapter below against the manga and flip verified to true. Until then the UI renders presence as unverified.
